title: Rymandra strobilina
intro: Rymandra strobilina, also known as Rauvolfia strobilina, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The health benefits of Rymandra strobilina include its potential to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and hypertension due to its sedative and hypotensive properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids such as ajmalicine, serpentine, and reserpine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rymandra strobilina are aqueous extracts and decoctions, which are traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Rymandra strobilina is a species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae. It belongs to the genus Rymandra, which is a small group of plants endemic to the Mascarene Islands. The Commelinaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes over 1,400 species. These plants are commonly known as spiderworts or dayflowers.
